mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20d returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recessionary pressures, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer and capital expenditure demand channels, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter through improved corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a positive 60d return in > 50% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month specifically during periods of stable inflation and accommodative monetary policy.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO trends yield negative 20d returns in > 55% of observations when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation remains persistently above 315 while the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the resulting stagflationary pressure forces aggressive portfolio de-risking, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns are positive in > 60% of periods where both thresholds are breached simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of positive nonfarm payrolls revisions and declining unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, which sustains consumer spending power and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between PAYEMS and UNRATE shows a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or fails to predict positive 60d returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth rates serve as a direct proxy for aggregate economic output and corporate earnings capacity, where higher GDPC1 values consistently map to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show no statistically significant correlation with 20d forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across the full sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month contractions in industrial production and retail sales signal a synchronized supply-demand breakdown, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as recession fears materialize.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1.0% MoM and RSAFS declines > 0.5% MoM
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5d returns in > 65% of instances where both supply and demand proxies decline simultaneously.
